content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 6,
1993
Howard Neal
Vice President and General Manager
KFI/KOST
610 South Ardmore Avenue
Los Angeles,
CA
90005
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—93--276
Dear Mr.
Neal:
You have requested advice on behalf of KFI,
Inc.
(“KFI”)
regarding provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”) •1
You are the Vice President and General Manager of KFI.
OUESTIONS
1.
Under the Act, is KFI(AM)
required to provide radio time for
the California Republican Party?
2.
Under the Act,
does KFI’s employment of Bill Press as a talk
show host constitute an “in—kind” contribution to the California
Democratic Party?
CONCLUSIONS
1.
There are no provisions in the Act which regulate the issue of
“equal time” of federally—regulated broadcast outlets.
You may
want to contact the Federal Communications Commission regarding
this issue.
2.
KFI’s employment of Bill Press as a talk show host does not
constitute an “in—kind” contribution to the California Democratic
Party.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000
g.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—93--276
Page
2
FACTS
KFI,
a Delaware corporation, is licensed by the Federal
Communications Commission
(“FCC”)
to operate radio stations
KFI(AM)
and KOST(FM)
in Los Angeles, California.
Radio Station
KFI(AM) presents a 24-hour all-talk format and employs a variety
of conunentator/hosts who represent a variety of viewpoints,
from
liberal to conservative.
The topics chosen, the persons
interviewed and the questions asked are selected by KFI, the
program producer and the program host,
who together retain sole
control over the programming.
The program host requests the listening audience to call in
with their views and will often describe differing positions in an
attempt to encourage listeners to call.
It is the usual practice
of each host to conduct his or her respective show in a manner
designed to promote discussion of all sides of an issue.
In January 1992,
KFI employed Bill Press to host a talk show
in the above—described manner.
On April 5,
1993,
Mr. Press became
Chair of the Califàrnia Democratic Party
(“CDP”),
which is a
volunteer unpaid position.
Mr. Press has not altered his program
or presentation since assuming this position and his program does
not differ from the general description of KFI’s programming.
KFI
neither promotes Mr. Press as the chair of the
CDP nor makes any
official station reference to Mr. Press’ position with the CDP.
KFI provides publicity for Mr.
Press’ talk show in the same manner
that it promotes the programs of its other hosts.
KFI has no agreement with the
CDP regarding any aspect of
KFI’s operations,
employment decisions or editorial policy.
KFI(AN) retains full and exclusive control over its programming
decisions.
Since Mr. Press assumed his position as chair of the CDP,
both the California Republican Party
(“CRP”)
and American
Independent Party
(“AlP”)
have demanded equal time on KFI(AM).
KFI has also been accused of providing an in—kind contribution of
airtime to the
CDP.
ANALYSIS
question i
There are no provisions in the Act which regulate the issue
of “equal time” of federally-regulated broadcast outlets.
Therefore, the Act would not require KFI to provide radio time for
the California Republican Party under the circumstances presented.
Please refer to the Sutton Advice Letter,
No.
1-92-473,
and the
Recht Advice Letter,
No.
1-91-044,
copies attached,
for a
discussion of free air time and under what circumstances this is a
reportable contribution.
You may want to contact the FCC to
determine if there are federal “equal time” requirements in this
situation.


File No.
A—93—276
Page
3
question
2
The question is whether KFI’s employment of the chair of the
CDP constitutes a contribution to the CDP.
Government Code
Section 82015 defines “contribution” as:
a payment,
a forgiveness of a loan,
a payment of
a loan by a third party,
or an enforceable promise
to make a payment except to the extent that full
and adequate consideration is received unless it is
clear from the surrounding circumstances that it is
not made for political purposes.
An expenditure
made at the behest of a candidate,
coimuittee or
elected officer is a contribution to the candidate,
committee or elected officer unless full and
adequate consideration is received for making the
expenditure.
(Emphasis added.)
Thus,
in order to-be construed as a contribution,
a payment
must be made for political purpàses.
Regulation 18215 provides
that a payment is made for political purposes if it is for the
purpose of influencing or attempting to influence the action of
the voters for or against the nomination or election of any
candidate or if the payment was received by or made at the behest
of the candidate,
committee or an organization formed primarily
for political purposes.
Regulation 18225 similarly defines when
-
an expenditure is made for political purposes.
There are no facts to indicate that KFI has made either a
contribution or an expenditure for political purposes
(Regulations
18215 and 18225).
The mere employment of a talk show host who
happens to be the chair of the
CDP constitutes neither a
contribution nor an expenditure to the CDP.
Please note that there is a specific exception in the
regulations which states that the term expenditure “does not
include costs incurred for communications which expressly advocate
the nomination, election or defeat of a clearly identified
candidate or candidates or the qualification, passage or defeat of
a clearly identified measure or measures by a federally regulated
broadcast outlet.
(Regulation l8225(b)(4)(B).
(Emphasis added.)
KFI(AM)
is a radio station regulated by the FCC.
Bill Press
is an employee and talk show host,
whose job is to encourage
discussion of political issues.
KFI has not made reportable
contributions or expenditures to the CDP by allowing its employee,
Bill Press, to make political commentary.


File No.
A—93—276
Page
4
I trust this answers your questions.
Sincerely,
Wayne Ordos
Executive Director
By:
C
nsel,
Legal Division
WO:JS:a]c
Enclosures
